# A simple sketchybar config for aerospace users to get started with
# Not too different from the base starting config!

PLUGIN_DIR="$CONFIG_DIR/plugins"
ITEM_DIR="$CONFIG_DIR/items" 
##### Bar Appearance #####
# Configuring the general appearance of the bar.
# These are only some of the options available. For all options see:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/bar
# If you are looking for other colors, see the color picker:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/tricks#color-picker

sketchybar --bar position=top height=40 blur_radius=30 color=0x40000000

##### Changing Defaults #####
# We now change some default values, which are applied to all further items.
# For a full list of all available item properties see:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/items

default=(
  padding_left=5
  padding_right=5
  icon.font="Hack Nerd Font:Bold:17.0"
  label.font="SF Pro:Semibold:17.0"
  icon.color=0xffffffff
  label.color=0xffffffff
  icon.padding_left=4
  icon.padding_right=4
  label.padding_left=4
  label.padding_right=4
  updates=on
)
sketchybar --default "${default[@]}"

##### Adding aeropsace layouts #####

# Add the aerospace_workspace_change event we specified in aerospace.toml
sketchybar --add event aerospace_workspace_change

# Add workspaces for all monitors
for monitor in $(aerospace list-monitors --format "%{monitor-appkit-nsscreen-screens-id}"); do
  for sid in $(aerospace list-workspaces --monitor "$monitor"); do
    # Determine which display this workspace should be shown on
    display_id="1"
    if [ "$sid" -ge 6 ] && [ "$sid" -le 7 ]; then
      display_id="2"
    fi
    
    sketchybar --add item space.$sid left \
      --set space.$sid display="$display_id" \
      --subscribe space.$sid aerospace_workspace_change \
      --set space.$sid \
      drawing=on \
      background.color=0x44ffffff \
      background.corner_radius=5 \
      background.drawing=on \
      background.border_color=0xAAFFFFFF \
      background.border_width=0 \
      background.height=25 \
      icon="$sid" \
      icon.padding_left=10 \
      icon.shadow.distance=4 \
      icon.shadow.color=0xA0000000 \
      label.font="sketchybar-app-font:Regular:16.0" \
      label.padding_right=20 \
      label.padding_left=0 \
      label.y_offset=-1 \
      label.shadow.drawing=off \
      label.shadow.color=0xA0000000 \
      label.shadow.distance=4 \
      click_script="aerospace workspace $sid" \
      script="$CONFIG_DIR/plugins/aerospace.sh $sid"
  done
done

# Load Icons on startup for all monitors
for monitor in $(aerospace list-monitors); do
  for sid in $(aerospace list-workspaces --monitor "$monitor" --empty no); do
    apps=$(aerospace list-windows --workspace "$sid" | awk -F'|' '{gsub(/^ *| *$/, "", $2); print $2}')

    sketchybar --set space.$sid drawing=on

    icon_strip=" "
    if [ "${apps}" != "" ]; then
      while read -r app; do
        icon_strip+=" $($CONFIG_DIR/plugins/icon_map_fn.sh "$app")"
      done <<<"${apps}"
    else
      icon_strip=""
    fi
    sketchybar --set space.$sid label="$icon_strip"
  done
done


#front app
source "$ITEM_DIR/front_app.sh"


##### Adding Right Items #####
# In the same way as the left items we can add items to the right side.
# Additional position (e.g. center) are available, see:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/items#adding-items-to-sketchybar

# Some items refresh on a fixed cycle, e.g. the clock runs its script once
# every 10s. Other gititems respond to events they subscribe to, e.g. the
# volume.sh script is only executed once an actual change in system audio
# volume is registered. More info about the event system can be found here:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/events

sketchybar --add item clock right \
  --set clock update_freq=10 script="$PLUGIN_DIR/clock.sh" \
  --add item volume right \
  --set volume script="$PLUGIN_DIR/volume.sh" \
  --subscribe volume volume_change \
  --add item battery right \
  --set battery update_freq=120 script="$PLUGIN_DIR/battery.sh" \
  --subscribe battery system_woke power_source_change \
  --add item swap e \
  icon="" \
  label.font="Hack Nerd Font:Italic:13.0" \
  icon.color=0x44FFFFFF \
  label.color=0x44FFFFFF

##### Force all scripts to run the first time (never do this in a script) #####
sketchybar --update

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# make sure it's executable with:
# chmod +x ~/.config/sketchybar/plugins/aerospace.sh

if [ "$1" = "$FOCUSED_WORKSPACE" ]; then
  sketchybar --set $NAME background.color=0x88FF00FF label.shadow.drawing=on icon.shadow.drawing=on background.border_width=2
else
  sketchybar --set $NAME background.color=0x44FFFFFF label.shadow.drawing=off icon.shadow.drawing=off background.border_width=0
fi

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issues. Let me analyze:

1. **Workspaces 6 and 7 not displayed**: In `sketchybarrc` line 45-47, workspaces 6 and 7 are set to `display_id="2"` (second monitor). If the user only has one monitor, those workspaces will be assigned to a non-existent display, so they won't show up.

2. **Ghostty icon not displayed**: Looking at `icon_map_fn.sh`, there's no case for "Ghostty". It falls through to the default case `*) icon_result=":default:"`. The `sketchybar-app-font` needs a mapping for Ghostty but it's not in the icon_map.
   
3. **Zen Browser icon not displayed**: There's a case for "Zen Browser" at line 697-698, but the app name as reported by aerospace might not match exactly what's in the icon map, or the font might be missing that glyph entirely.

Looking at the user's report more carefully - Slack works but Ghostty and Zen don't. Ghostty has no entry in the icon map at all, so it defaults to the generic icon. For Zen Browser, even though there's a mapping to `:zen_browser:`, the sketchybar-app-font might not actually contain that glyph, which would result in a blank space instead of an icon. me check if there's a `:ghostty:` icon in the sketchybar-app-font. The sketchybar-app-font is a community-maintained font. Ghostty is relatively new, so it might not be in older versions.

Let me summarize for the user:

1. **Workspaces 6, 7**: They're hardcoded to `display_id="2"` (second monitor). If only one monitor is connected, they won't appear.

2. **Ghostty**: No entry in `icon_map_fn.sh` - it falls to `:default:`. Need to add a "Ghostty" case.

3. **Zen Browser**: There IS an entry for "Zen Browser" → `:zen_browser:`. The issue might be that either the app reports a different name than "Zen Browser" or the sketchybar-app-font version doesn't include the `:zen_browser:` glyph.

content.text:
なるほどっすね、原因わかったっすわ。3つの問題があるっす。

## 1. Workspace 6, 7 が表示されない

`sketchybarrc:45-47` で、workspace 6と7は `display_id="2"`（2台目のモニター）にハードコードされてるっす。

```bash
if [ "$sid" -ge 6 ] && [ "$sid" -le 7 ]; then
  display_id="2"
fi
```

モニター1台しか繋いでなかったら、display 2は存在しないんで表示されないっすよ。

## 2. Ghostty のアイコンが表示されない

`plugins/icon_map_fn.sh` に **Ghostty のエントリが存在しない**っす。`case` 文にマッチしないから `*) icon_result=":default:"` に落ちてるっすね。

## 3. Zen Browser のアイコンが表示されない

`icon_map_fn.sh:697` に `"Zen Browser"` → `:zen_browser:` のマッピングは**ある**っす。考えられる原因は：
- aerospaceが報告するアプリ名が `"Zen Browser"` と一致してない（例: `"Zen"` とか `"zen"` とか）
- インストールしてる `sketchybar-app-font` のバージョンが古くて `:zen_browser:` グリフが含まれてない

確認してみるっすか？

Based on the search results, here's information about sketchybar-app-font icon map and related tools:

## sketchybar-app-font Overview

The sketchybar-app-font is a ligature-based symbol font and a mapping function for sketchybar, inspired by simple-bar's usage of community-contributed minimalistic app icons.

## Icon Map System

The icon mapping works through mapping files that associate app names with glyphs:
- You add a file to `/mappings/` using the same name but without the '.svg' extension, which indicates which app names should match the icon.
- An icon_map.lua file is created in `~/.config/sketchybar/helpers/` during the build process.

## Creating Glyphs

Icons can be obtained directly or redrawn in Figma if not in vector format, with no need for extreme precision as it's displayed in a really small size, and all solid shapes will become part of the glyph.

## Regarding Ghostty

The search results do not contain specific information about Ghostty glyph support or mappings within sketchybar-app-font as of 2025-2026. The results show various sketchybar-app-font implementations but do not include recent updates about Ghostty integration.

Based on the repository structure, there are **449 mapping files** for various applications.

**Ghostty:** Yes, there is a mapping file: `:ghostty:`

**Zen Browser:** Yes, there is a mapping file: `:zen_browser:`

Additionally, the repository includes a separate entry for `:zed:` (the Zed editor), but no standalone "Zen" mapping exists—only the Zen Browser variant is present in the collection.

最新版は **v2.0.53** (2025-02-04) っす。こうやって更新するっすよ。

```bash
curl -L https://github.com/kvndrsslr/sketchybar-app-font/releases/latest/download/sketchybar-app-font.ttf -o ~/Library/Fonts/sketchybar-app-font.ttf

その後 sketchybar を再起動っす。

sketchybar --reload
```

# A simple sketchybar config for aerospace users to get started with
# Not too different from the base starting config!

PLUGIN_DIR="$CONFIG_DIR/plugins"
ITEM_DIR="$CONFIG_DIR/items" 
##### Bar Appearance #####
# Configuring the general appearance of the bar.
# These are only some of the options available. For all options see:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/bar
# If you are looking for other colors, see the color picker:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/tricks#color-picker

sketchybar --bar position=top height=40 blur_radius=30 color=0x40000000

##### Changing Defaults #####
# We now change some default values, which are applied to all further items.
# For a full list of all available item properties see:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/items

default=(
  padding_left=5
  padding_right=5
  icon.font="Hack Nerd Font:Bold:17.0"
  label.font="SF Pro:Semibold:17.0"
  icon.color=0xffffffff
  label.color=0xffffffff
  icon.padding_left=4
  icon.padding_right=4
  label.padding_left=4
  label.padding_right=4
  updates=on
)
sketchybar --default "${default[@]}"

##### Adding aeropsace layouts #####

# Add the aerospace_workspace_change event we specified in aerospace.toml
sketchybar --add event aerospace_workspace_change

# Add workspaces for all monitors
for monitor in $(aerospace list-monitors --format "%{monitor-appkit-nsscreen-screens-id}"); do
  for sid in $(aerospace list-workspaces --monitor "$monitor"); do
    sketchybar --add item space.$sid left \
      --subscribe space.$sid aerospace_workspace_change \
      --set space.$sid \
      drawing=on \
      background.color=0x44ffffff \
      background.corner_radius=5 \
      background.drawing=on \
      background.border_color=0xAAFFFFFF \
      background.border_width=0 \
      background.height=25 \
      icon="$sid" \
      icon.padding_left=10 \
      icon.shadow.distance=4 \
      icon.shadow.color=0xA0000000 \
      label.font="sketchybar-app-font:Regular:16.0" \
      label.padding_right=20 \
      label.padding_left=0 \
      label.y_offset=-1 \
      label.shadow.drawing=off \
      label.shadow.color=0xA0000000 \
      label.shadow.distance=4 \
      click_script="aerospace workspace $sid" \
      script="$CONFIG_DIR/plugins/aerospace.sh $sid"
  done
done

# Load Icons on startup for all monitors
for monitor in $(aerospace list-monitors); do
  for sid in $(aerospace list-workspaces --monitor "$monitor" --empty no); do
    apps=$(aerospace list-windows --workspace "$sid" | awk -F'|' '{gsub(/^ *| *$/, "", $2); print $2}')

    sketchybar --set space.$sid drawing=on

    icon_strip=" "
    if [ "${apps}" != "" ]; then
      while read -r app; do
        icon_strip+=" $($CONFIG_DIR/plugins/icon_map_fn.sh "$app")"
      done <<<"${apps}"
    else
      icon_strip=""
    fi
    sketchybar --set space.$sid label="$icon_strip"
  done
done


#front app
source "$ITEM_DIR/front_app.sh"


##### Adding Right Items #####
# In the same way as the left items we can add items to the right side.
# Additional position (e.g. center) are available, see:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/items#adding-items-to-sketchybar

# Some items refresh on a fixed cycle, e.g. the clock runs its script once
# every 10s. Other gititems respond to events they subscribe to, e.g. the
# volume.sh script is only executed once an actual change in system audio
# volume is registered. More info about the event system can be found here:
# https://felixkratz.github.io/SketchyBar/config/events

sketchybar --add item clock right \
  --set clock update_freq=10 script="$PLUGIN_DIR/clock.sh" \
  --add item volume right \
  --set volume script="$PLUGIN_DIR/volume.sh" \
  --subscribe volume volume_change \
  --add item battery right \
  --set battery update_freq=120 script="$PLUGIN_DIR/battery.sh" \
  --subscribe battery system_woke power_source_change \
  --add item swap e \
  icon="" \
  label.font="Hack Nerd Font:Italic:13.0" \
  icon.color=0x44FFFFFF \
  label.color=0x44FFFFFF

##### Force all scripts to run the first time (never do this in a script) #####
sketchybar --update

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# make sure it's executable with:
# chmod +x ~/.config/sketchybar/plugins/aerospace.sh

if [ "$1" = "$FOCUSED_WORKSPACE" ]; then
  sketchybar --set $NAME background.color=0x88FF00FF label.shadow.drawing=on icon.shadow.drawing=on background.border_width=2
else
  sketchybar --set $NAME background.color=0x44FFFFFF label.shadow.drawing=off icon.shadow.drawing=off background.border_width=0
fi

了解っす。現在の色とTokyo Nightのカラーを整理するっすね。

**現在の色（全部白/グレー系の半透明）：**
- バー背景: `0x40000000`（黒の薄い半透明）
- workspace背景: `0x44ffffff`（白の薄い半透明）
- フォーカス時: `0x88FF00FF`（マゼンタ半透明）
- ボーダー: `0xAAFFFFFF`（白）

**Tokyo Night のカラーパレット：**
- bg: `#1a1b26`
- fg: `#c0caf5`
- blue: `#7aa2f7`（アクセント）
- purple: `#bb9af7`
- cyan: `#7dcfff`

sketchybarは `0xAARRGGBB` 形式っす。変更するっすよ。

content.text:
できたっす。Tokyo Nightカラーに変更したっすよ。

## カラーマッピング

| 要素 | Before | After (Tokyo Night) |
|------|--------|---------------------|
| **バー背景** | `0x40000000` (黒半透明) | `0xE01a1b26` (bg) |
| **テキスト/アイコン** | `0xffffffff` (白) | `0xffc0caf5` (fg) |
| **workspace背景** | `0x44ffffff` (白半透明) | `0x4424283b` (surface) |
| **workspaceボーダー** | `0xAAFFFFFF` (白) | `0xAA414868` (border) |
| **フォーカス中** | `0x88FF00FF` (マゼンタ) | `0x887aa2f7` (blue) |
| **セパレーター** | `0x44FFFFFF` (白) | `0x44565f89` (comment) |
